title: Unify Bureau canonical runtime and Grabowski contract deployment
goal: Remove the remaining dual-deployment model by making Grabowski's Bureau lease consumer verify and execute the same immutable manifest-bound release selected by the canonical Bureau launcher, without weakening package integrity, exact-scope lease policy or rollback safety.

acceptance.assertion: The canonical Bureau launcher and Grabowski lease consumer resolve one manifest, one source commit, one immutable package-tree digest and one rollback chain; no separately built venv is required for normal operation.

Grabowski still verifies the exact contract executable or interpreter, bureau.cli, bureau.lease_contract and the complete allowed package set before and after invocation, with source commit and manifest digest in the receipt.

Exact path and object scopes remain healthy, broad normal Bureau repository leases remain forbidden, emergency recovery boundaries remain unchanged and no manifest result grants lease authority by itself.
A create-first migration switches Grabowski only after a positive shadow comparison against the existing d85ad423 contract venv and retains both the previous selector and manifest for rollback.
Missing, malformed, symlinked, digest-mismatched, stale-commit or concurrently changed manifests and package trees fail closed while bounded diagnostics remain available.

Tests cover the current compatibility bridge, canonical manifest success, drift, races, rollback and policy parity; a deployed live proof acquires and releases one exact synthetic Bureau scope and rejects one broad normal repo scope for the policy reason rather than runtime drift.

current_state.runtime_safety: Both surfaces are independently hash-bound to the same source commit and currently pass live probes.
current_state.remaining_cost: Two release builders, two rollback models and two runtime selectors must be maintained.

metadata.safety_boundary: Do not remove, overwrite or retarget the existing contract venv until manifest parity, exact-scope behavior and rollback are proven on the deployed Grabowski runtime.
